data.title: Bitcoin Featured on Financial Times Front Page
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Bitcoin has made headlines by appearing on the front page of the Financial Times. The article, titled 'Why Public Companies Are Flocking to Buy Bitcoin,' explores the growing trend of publicly traded companies investing in the cryptocurrency. This development highlights the increasing interest and adoption of Bitcoin in the corporate sector.
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/28040100761314?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: First 2x Leveraged Galaxy ETF Begins Trading
data.description: According to PANews, Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas announced that the first 2x leveraged Galaxy ETF (GLXU) has officially commenced trading today.

data.title: H100 Group AB to Join MSCI Global Micro Cap Index in August
data.description: According to PANews, Swedish-listed company H100 Group AB, with stock codes $H100 and $GS9, will be included in the MSCI Global Micro Cap Index following the latest MSCI index review. This change will take effect on August 26, 2025.

data.title: U.S. Treasury yields hold steady in the third straight week of weak Treasury auctions
data.description:
U. S. Treasuries remained little changed in the third straight week of weak Treasury auctions as investors weighed the effects of Trump’s tariffs.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/treasury-yield-steady-third-week-of-auctions/

data.title: Ripple To Gobble Up Payments Platform Rail for $200,000,000 To Support Transactions via XRP and RLUSD Stablecoin
data.description:
The blockchain payments firm Ripple says it has agreed to acquire the stablecoin-powered payment infrastructure platform Rail for $200 million. In a statement, Ripple says it is acquiring the Toronto-based Rail amid rising demand for transactions involving stablecoins – cryptocurrencies pegged to another asset such as the US dollar.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/08/08/ripple-to-gobble-up-payments-platform-rail-for-200000000-to-support-transactions-via-xrp-and-rlusd-stablecoin/

data.title: International Business Settlement Announces Joint Venture Agreement
data.description: According to Foresight News, International Business Settlement (00147.HK) has announced a joint venture agreement between its wholly-owned subsidiary, Cheng Tai, and Mr. Chen Sanming. The agreement, signed on August 8, 2025, outlines the formation of a new enterprise, with Cheng Tai holding a 70% stake and Mr. Chen Sanming owning 30%. The joint venture will primarily focus on activities related to cryptocurrency assets.

data.title: LM Funding America Reports Increased Bitcoin Production and Energy Revenue
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Nasdaq-listed LM Funding America (LMFA) has released its operational data for July 2025. During this period, the company reported a net production of 5.9 bitcoins, marking a 7% increase compared to the previous month. Additionally, the company's energy revenue saw a 20% rise.

As of July 31, LM Funding America holds 150.4 bitcoins, with a total estimated value of approximately $17.8 million.

data.title: TON Blockchain Data Now Accessible on AWS
data.description: According to Foresight News, TON has announced that its dataset is now available on Amazon Web Services (AWS) public blockchain datasets. This initiative allows researchers, developers, and analysts to access TON blockchain data free of charge.

data.title: Canada's Employment Decline Sparks Rate Cut Speculation
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Canada's employment figures for July showed a decrease of 40,800 jobs, contrary to the expected increase of 13,500 jobs. The previous month's employment change was an increase of 83,100 jobs. Following the release of this data, the Canadian swap market indicated a 38% probability of a rate cut by the Bank of Canada in September, up from 33% before the employment data was announced.

data.title: Traders bet on $200K year-end Bitcoin, but real odds tell a different story
data.description:
Despite aggressive bullish bets, market odds imply under 3% chance of $200,000 BTC price by December of this year.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/traders-bet-on-200k-year-end-bitcoin-but-real-odds-tell-different-story?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: XRP Futures Trading Volume Surges, Overtakes SOL
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, XRP futures trading volume has experienced a significant increase, rising by 208% within 24 hours to reach $12.4 billion, surpassing SOL's $9.6 billion. Open interest has climbed to $5.9 billion, marking a 15% increase.

Glassnode analysis indicates that the current market funding rates suggest a predominance of long positions. This scenario could heighten liquidation risks if prices decline.

data.title: Bitcoin Options Expiry Approaches Amid Strategic Trading Moves
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitcoin (BTC) traders are preparing for the significant year-end options expiry, set for December 26 at 8:00 am UTC. This event involves $8.8 billion in options, with over $1 billion becoming active if Bitcoin surpasses $200,000. However, the likelihood of such a rally remains low, with less than a 3% chance of profit for $200,000 call options. The current open interest for call options stands at $6.45 billion, while put options trail at $2.36 billion, indicating a preference for bullish positions. Despite this, bearish traders seem comfortable with Bitcoin staying below $120,000.

Professional traders employ strategies like the Call Diagonal Spread, which involves buying a $200,000 December call and selling a $200,000 call with an earlier expiry, typically in October. This strategy profits if Bitcoin exceeds $146,000 by October 31, allowing the long-dated call to appreciate while the short-term call expires worthless. However, Bitcoin prices above $200,000 could negatively impact this strategy, with potential losses capped at BTC 0.005, approximately $585 at current prices, and maximum gains at BTC 0.0665, roughly $7,750.

Another strategy, the Inverse Call Butterfly, involves buying one $140,000 call, selling two $160,000 calls, and buying one $200,000 call—all with December expiries. This position profits most if Bitcoin lands near $160,000 on December 26, netting BTC 0.112, around $13,050. Losses begin if Bitcoin climbs past $178,500, though the $200,000 call helps cap potential losses at 0.109 BTC, or approximately $12,700.

Despite the sizable open interest in $200,000 call options, traders are not necessarily expecting Bitcoin to reach that level. Nearly $900 million in put options are positioned between $50,000 and $80,000 for the December expiry, indicating bearish bets are also in play, albeit with lower odds. The $140,000 call is priced around BTC 0.051, implying a 21% probability based on the Black-Scholes model, while the $200,000 call trades at BTC 0.007, reflecting an implied probability below 3%. These aggressive strike prices may attract attention, but the data suggests traders are using far-out-of-the-money calls within structured strategies that offer limited risk and leveraged upside. According to Polymarket, the odds of Bitcoin reaching $200,000 this year are higher at 13%.

data.title: Animoca Brands Acquires Majority Stake in Cool Cats Group
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Animoca Brands and its affiliated companies have announced the completion of a strategic investment in the NFT brand Cool Cats Group. Following this investment, Animoca Brands and Coin Operated Group will hold the majority ownership of Cool Cats Group. The collaboration aims to drive innovation and growth, enhance community engagement, and explore new market opportunities in the rapidly evolving NFT sector. As part of the investment, Animoca Brands will appoint two new board members to advance Cool Cats' development: Yat Siu, co-founder and executive chairman of Animoca Brands, and Kenneth Shek, project lead for Mocaverse and Moca Network
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/28038868710969?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS
